title: cotula sericea var. concolor
intro: Cotula sericea var. concolor, also known as White Buttons or White Dusty Miller, is a variety of the Cotula sericea plant, a type of flowering plant in the daisy family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the sesquiterpene lactones present in its leaves. The health benefits of Cotula sericea var. concolor include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
